options_section_templates_html.message:
Templates

Configure title, front-matter, and back-matter templates for your clipped markdown files.

Template variable reference
For the title, as well as the front- and back-matter custom text, you can use the following text replacement values. Please note that not all websites will provide all values.
  • {title} — Article Title
  • {pageTitle} — Title of the actual page
  • {length} — Length of the article, in characters
  • {excerpt} — Article description or short excerpt from the content
  • {byline} — Author metadata
  • {dir} — Content direction
  • {date:FORMAT} — The current date and time. Check the format reference
  • {keywords} — Meta keywords (if present). Comma separated by default.
  • {keywords:SEPARATOR} — Meta keywords separated by SEPARATOR. For example, to separate by space, use {keywords: }
There is also support for all meta tags not mentioned above, should the page you are clipping support them. For example, try {og:image} or any other widely supported meta tags.

URL information:

  • {baseURI} - Parsed document/base URI (legacy behavior).
  • {pageURL} / {tabURL} - Current address-bar URL of the tab.
  • {origin} - The origin from {baseURI} (scheme + domain + port).
  • {host} - The host from {baseURI} (hostname, and port if present).
  • {hostname} - The hostname from {baseURI}.
  • {port} - The port number from {baseURI}.
  • {protocol} - The protocol scheme from {baseURI}, including the trailing :.
  • {pathname} - The path from {baseURI}, not including query string or fragment.
  • {search} - The query string from {baseURI}, including ?.
  • {hash} - The fragment identifier from {baseURI}, including #.
  • {pageOrigin}, {pageHost}, {pageHostname}, {pagePort}, {pageProtocol}, {pagePathname}, {pageSearch}, {pageHash} - Same URL components as above, but from {pageURL}.

Examples:

  • SPA route example (Gemini style):
  • Address bar URL: https://gemini.google.com/app/19a994212aad751c?hl=en#chat
  • Parsed/base URI: https://gemini.google.com/
  • {baseURI} -> https://gemini.google.com/
  • {pageURL} -> https://gemini.google.com/app/19a994212aad751c?hl=en#chat
  • {pathname} -> /, {pagePathname} -> /app/19a994212aad751c
  • {search} -> (empty), {pageSearch} -> ?hl=en
  • {hash} -> (empty), {pageHash} -> #chat
  • Standard page example (no special base behavior):
  • Address bar URL: https://example.com/blog/post?ref=md#top
  • {baseURI} and {pageURL} are usually the same.

Template snippets:

  • source: {pageURL} (recommended for current tab URL)
  • source_base: {baseURI} (document/base URI)
  • source_parts: {pageOrigin}{pagePathname}{pageSearch}{pageHash}

Additionally, you can 'parameterize' any of the text variables (other than date and keywords) by using the following syntax:

  • {variable:pascal} — PascalCase: Every word capital
  • {variable:camel} — camelCase: every word capital except the first word
  • {variable:kebab} — kebab-case: hyphens between words, all lowercase
  • {variable:snake} — snake_case: underscores between words, all lowercase

Title template

Clips folder

Disallowed characters

Characters to strip from title/filename — in addition to /, ?, <, >, \, :, *, |, "

Front-matter template

Live preview Raw output

Preview uses example metadata and the current date/time.


                            

                        

                    

                


                
                

                    
Back-matter template

                    

                        
                        
                        

                            
                                
                                    Live preview
                                    Raw output
                                
                            
                            

                                
Preview uses example metadata and the current date/time.

                                

                            

                        

                    

                


                
                

                    

                        

                            
                            
Same as the popup's Template toggle. When enabled, each clip includes your Front-matter and Back-matter templates.

options_section_appearance_html.message:
Appearance

Customize the look of the MarkSnip popup.

Interface Language

Choose the language used by the popup, settings, guide, shortcuts modal, and in-extension notifications.

Manifest-level strings such as the extension name, action title, and command descriptions still follow the browser locale.

Color Mode

Adds a header button in the popup for one-click switching between light and dark mode.

Special Themes

Apply a tailored aesthetic across the popup, options page, guide, and editor.

Uses a WCAG AA palette across the popup, options page, guide, and editor.

Display the mascot icon in the header when using supported special themes.

Accent Color

The active special theme controls accent color while active.

Editor Theme

Choose a syntax highlighting theme for the Markdown editor in the popup.

The active special theme provides its own light and dark editor themes while active.

Hides quick settings panel and reduces padding for a minimal popup. The settings icon remains accessible in the header.

Displays the User Guide button in the popup header for quick access to help.

options_section_downloads_html.message:
Downloads

Configure how files are downloaded and integrate with Obsidian.

Shows right-click actions for clipping pages, selected text, links, and tabs.

Shows the Batch Processing entry in the popup and allows bulk URL conversion.

Download Mode

Method to use for downloading Markdown files. Set to "Content Link" if you're having trouble with the Downloads API (Sometimes conflicts can occur with other download extensions, leading to randomly generated filenames and other symptoms).

Note: "Content Link" mode disables some functionality such as downloading images or using subfolders in the filename.

Default Popup Action

Controls the popup's main action button and selection action button only. Context menus, shortcuts, batch exports, library exports, Agent Bridge, and Obsidian remain Markdown-based.

Default Assistant Target

Used when the popup's main action is set to Send to Assistant. If a selected custom target is removed, MarkSnip falls back to ChatGPT.

Assistant Targets

ChatGPT, Claude, and Perplexity are built in. Add custom HTTPS URLs that accept a {prompt} placeholder in the query string.

Use exactly one {prompt} placeholder. Example: https://example.com/new?q={prompt}

Assistant URL Length Cap

Advanced setting for Send to Assistant links. If a generated assistant URL exceeds this value, MarkSnip copies the Markdown to your clipboard and opens the assistant landing page instead. Default: 3600. Changing this is not recommended.

Regardless of browser setting

In Downloads API mode, image files are saved too and markdown image links are rewritten to those local files.

Obsidian Integration

For integration with Obsidian, you need to install and enable the community plugin Advanced Obsidian URI. This plugin helps bypass character limitations in URLs by using the clipboard as the source for creating new files. Images sent this way stay as remote links; attachment files are not copied into your vault. More information →

Permissions & Privacy

MarkSnip processes pages locally and only accesses page content when you trigger a clip.

Site access for clipping Included with extension
  • Needed so MarkSnip can clip any page you ask it to clip.
  • Not used for passive browsing monitoring.
  • Page content is not sent to external servers.
  • This is managed by the browser as part of the extension install.
Local companion access Optional
  • Only used for Agent Bridge.
  • Only talks to the local MarkSnip companion installed on this machine.
  • Regular clipping works without it.
  • Can be stopped by turning Agent Bridge off.

Agent Bridge

Install the local MarkSnip companion once, then let local tools pull the current page's Markdown directly without using downloads.

When off, MarkSnip will not open a local companion connection.

Enable local companion access?

MarkSnip is about to ask the browser for permission to talk to the local MarkSnip companion.

  • Only for Agent Bridge
  • Local machine only
  • Regular clipping still works without this
View setup guide

Permission wasn't granted. Agent Bridge stays off. Regular clipping is unchanged.

Status
Disabled

Enable the Agent Bridge to let MarkSnip connect to the local companion.

Setup guide

To connect MarkSnip to local CLI tools, follow these steps:

  1. Download the companion archive for your OS from the Releases page.
  2. Run the install command to register the native messaging host:
$ marksnip install-host
  1. Enable the toggle above and MarkSnip will connect automatically.

Local-only feature. Bridge settings are not exported and do not sync between machines.

options_section_library_html.message:
Library

Manage the popup Library that stores saved clips locally on this browser only.

Shows the Library view in the popup. Turning it off hides the feature without deleting saved items.

When off, the popup Library shows a manual Save Clip button instead.

Items to Keep

Maximum number of saved Library clips to keep locally. Lowering this trims older items immediately.

Library settings are included in settings import/export. Saved Library items remain local-only and are excluded.

Clear Library

Delete all saved Library items from local storage. This cannot be undone.

options_section_markdown_html.message:
Markdown

Configure how HTML is converted to Markdown syntax.

Heading Style

Horizontal Rule

Bullet List Marker

Code Block Style

When enabled, preserves original HTML formatting within code blocks. This can be useful for maintaining exact visual formatting, but may make the Markdown harder to read and use in other tools. When disabled (default), produces clean code blocks.

Uses highlight.js to infer fenced code block language when no explicit language is present. Disable this if snippets are frequently labeled with incorrect languages.

Code Block Fence

Emphasis (italics) Delimiter

Strong (bold) Delimiter

Link Style

Link Reference Style

Image Style

Note: The following only apply if Download Images is on.

Image Reference Style

Note: The following only apply if Image Style is markdown (not Obsidian-styled).

By default, backslashes (\) are used to escape Markdown characters in the HTML input. This ensures that these characters are not interpreted as Markdown. For example, the contents of <h1>1. Hello world</h1> needs to be escaped to 1\. Hello world, otherwise it will be interpreted as a list item rather than a heading. Disabling this option disables this escaping.

Hashtag Handling

Control how hashtag-like words (for example #AI) are written in clipped text. Useful for tools like Obsidian that treat hashtags as tags.

Example: #research becomes research in Remove #, or \#research in Escape #.

options_section_tables_html.message:
Tables

Configure how HTML tables are converted to Markdown tables.

Removes all links from tables, keeping only the text content. Creates cleaner, more readable tables.

| Name              |
|-------------------|
| [Google](http://…) |
| Name   |
|--------|
| Google |

Removes formatting like bold and italic from table cells. Results in plain text tables.

| Item       | Note     |
|------------|----------|
| **Apple**  | _Fresh_  |
| Item   | Note  |
|--------|-------|
| Apple  | Fresh |

Formats tables with proper spacing and alignment. Makes tables more readable in markdown.

|Name|Price|
|---|---|
|Apple|$1.50|
|Banana|$0.75|
| Name   | Price |
|--------|-------|
| Apple  | $1.50 |
| Banana | $0.75 |

Centers text within table columns. Creates a more polished look.

| Name   | Price |
|--------|-------|
| Apple  | $1.50 |
| Name   | Price |
|:------:|:-----:|
| Apple  | $1.50 |

options_section_site_rules_html.message:
Site Rules

Create ordered wildcard URL rules that override clip settings for matching sites. The first enabled match wins.

Rule List

Use patterns like example.com/*, docs.example.com/blog/*, or *.example.com/*. Query strings and hashes are ignored.

0 rules
No site rules yet.

Add a rule when you want different templates, image behavior, or table formatting for specific domains.

Add Site Rule

Missing scheme matches both http and https. Missing path implies /*.

Disabled rules stay in the list but never match.

Boolean Overrides
Template Overrides
Image Overrides
Table Formatting Overrides

guide_section_quick_start_html.message:
#Quick Start

  1. Click the MarkSnip icon in the browser toolbar to open the popup.
  2. The current page is run through Mozilla Readability to extract the main content, then converted to Markdown with Turndown.
  3. Edit, copy, or download the Markdown using the built-in CodeMirror editor.
Tip: Use Alt+Shift+M to open the popup without clicking the icon.

guide_section_using_the_popup_html.message:
#Using the Popup

#Quick Settings

At the top of the popup you'll find two toggle switches:

  • Images — when on, images are downloaded alongside your Markdown file (requires Downloads API mode).
  • Template — when on, the front-matter and back-matter templates are prepended/appended to the output.

#Clipping Selection vs. Full Document

Below the toggles is a segmented control with two buttons:

  • Selection — clips only the text you selected on the page before opening the popup.
  • Document — clips the entire page content (default if nothing is selected).

#Editing the Title

The Title field determines the filename of the downloaded Markdown file. It is populated automatically from the title template (configurable in options) but you can edit it freely before saving.

#Markdown Preview

The main area of the popup is a syntax-highlighted Markdown editor (powered by CodeMirror). You can make quick edits here before downloading.

Above the editor are two buttons:

  • Copy All — copies all the Markdown to your clipboard.
  • Copy Selection — appears when you select text inside the editor, allowing you to copy just the highlighted portion.

#Action Buttons

ButtonAction
Main ActionUses your configured popup default action. Choose Markdown (.md), Plain text (.txt), HTML (.html), PDF, or Send to Assistant.
Selection ActionAppears when text is selected inside the editor; exports or sends only the selected text using the same popup default action.
Send to ObsidianCopies Markdown to clipboard and opens Obsidian via the Advanced URI plugin to create a new note (only visible when Obsidian integration is enabled).

Note: This popup default action setting only affects popup actions. Context menus, keyboard download shortcuts, batch exports, Library exports, Agent Bridge, and Obsidian actions remain Markdown-based.

guide_section_batch_processing_html.message:
#Batch Processing

Click the batch icon in the popup header to switch to batch mode.

#Adding URLs

Paste URLs into the text area, one per line. You can also use Markdown link syntax:

https://example.com/page1
https://example.com/page2
[Page Title](https://example.com/page3)

#Pick Links from Page

Click Pick Links from Page to enter the visual link picker. This injects an overlay onto the current page that lets you click on links to select them. A floating toolbar shows how many links you've selected, and a Done button sends them back to the batch URL list.

#Output Format

  • ZIP file — all converted Markdown files are bundled into a single .zip download.
  • Individual — each page is downloaded as a separate .md file.

Click Convert All URLs to start processing. A progress bar shows the current status including a count and the URL being processed.

guide_section_context_menus_html.message:
#Context Menus & Shortcuts

Right-click on a page to access MarkSnip actions under the context menu. These are available when Enable Context Menus is turned on in settings.

#Download Actions

Menu ItemContextDescription
Download Tab as MarkdownPage / TabDownloads the current tab as a Markdown file without opening the popup.
Download Selection as MarkdownSelected textDownloads the highlighted text as Markdown.
Download All Tabs as MarkdownPage / TabDownloads every open tab in the current window as Markdown files.

#Copy Actions

Menu ItemDescription
Copy Tab as MarkdownConverts the entire page to Markdown and copies it to the clipboard.
Copy Selection as MarkdownConverts the selection to Markdown and copies it.
Copy Link as MarkdownCopies the right-clicked link as [text](url).
Copy Image as MarkdownCopies the right-clicked image as ![alt](src).
Copy Tab URL as Markdown LinkCopies the tab's URL as [title](url).
Copy All Tab URLs as Markdown Link ListCopies all open tab URLs as a Markdown link list.
Copy Selected Tab URLs as Markdown Link ListCopies URLs from multi-selected tabs (Ctrl/Cmd) as a link list.

#Obsidian Actions

These items appear only when Obsidian integration is enabled:

Menu ItemDescription
Send Text Selection to ObsidianSends the selection to Obsidian as a new note.
Send Tab to ObsidianSends the full page to Obsidian as a new note.

At the bottom of the context menu you'll also find checkboxes for Include front/back template and Download Images.

#Keyboard Shortcuts

ShortcutAction
Alt+Shift+MOpen the MarkSnip popup
Alt+Shift+DDownload current tab as Markdown
Alt+Shift+CCopy current tab as Markdown
Alt+Shift+LCopy current tab URL as Markdown link

Additional commands without default shortcuts (assign your own in the browser's shortcut settings):

  • Copy current selection as Markdown
  • Copy selected tabs as Markdown link list
  • Copy current selection to Obsidian
  • Copy current tab to Obsidian

guide_section_settings_html.message:
#Settings & Customization

Open the options page by clicking the gear icon in the popup header, or by right-clicking the MarkSnip icon and selecting Options.

#Templates

Title Template

Controls the popup's title field and the resulting filename. Uses Custom Text Substitutions. Default: {pageTitle}

Downloads Subfolder

A subfolder within your browser's downloads folder where Markdown files are saved. Only available in Downloads API mode. Uses text substitutions.

Disallowed Characters

Characters automatically stripped from filenames, in addition to system-reserved characters. Default: []#^ (for Obsidian compatibility).

Front/Back Templates

Text prepended or appended to every clipped Markdown file. Useful for YAML front-matter or other metadata. Uses text substitutions.

---
created: {date:YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss} (UTC {date:Z})
tags: [{keywords}]
source: {pageURL}
author: {byline}
---

#Downloads

Context Menus

Toggles the right-click context menu items on and off. Default: On.

Default Popup Action

Controls the popup's primary action buttons. Choose Markdown, Plain text, HTML, PDF, or Send to Assistant. This does not change context-menu downloads, keyboard download shortcuts, batch output, Library exports, Agent Bridge output, or Obsidian sends.

Default Assistant Target

When Send to Assistant is selected, choose the popup's default destination such as ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, or one of your custom assistant URLs.

Assistant Targets

ChatGPT, Claude, and Perplexity are built in. Add custom HTTPS assistant URLs that accept a {prompt} placeholder in the query string. MarkSnip inserts the current Markdown into that placeholder when you send from the popup.

Assistant URL Length Cap

Controls how long a generated assistant URL can be before MarkSnip falls back to copying the Markdown to your clipboard and opening the assistant landing page instead. The default is 3600, and changing it is not recommended.

#Markdown Options

Heading Style

Setext uses underlines (=== / ---); Atx (default) uses # prefixes.

Horizontal Rule Style

Choose between ***, ---, or ___ (default).

Bullet List Marker

*, - (default), or +.

Code Block Style

Indented (four-space indent) or Fenced (default, wrapped in ``` or ~~~).

Preserve Code Block HTML Formatting

When enabled, preserves the original HTML formatting inside code blocks. Default: Off.

Emphasis & Strong Delimiters

Italics: _ (default), *, or __ (for Roam). Bold: ** (default) or __.

Link Style

Inlined (default): [Google](http://google.com). Referenced: separate link definitions at the bottom. Strip Links: removes links entirely.

Image Style

StyleOutputRequires Download Images
Original Source![](http://example.com/img.jpg)No
Strip Images(image removed)No
Pure Markdown (default)![](folder/image.jpg)Yes
Base64 Encoded![](data:image/png;base64,...)Yes
Obsidian Internal Embed![[folder/image.jpg]]Yes
Obsidian Embed (no folder)![[image.jpg]]Yes

Escape Markdown Characters

Backslash-escapes special Markdown characters to prevent misinterpretation. Default: On.

Hashtag Handling

Keep (default), Remove #, or Escape #. Only affects article text; Markdown syntax and code are preserved.

#Table Formatting Options

OptionDescriptionDefault
Strip Links from TablesRemoves hyperlinks from table cells, keeping only the text.On
Strip FormattingRemoves bold, italic, and other formatting from table cells.Off
Pretty Print TablesAdds proper spacing and column alignment.On
Center Text in ColumnsCenters text within table columns.On

#Import / Export Settings

  • Import — restore settings from a previously exported JSON file.
  • Export — save all current settings to a JSON file for backup or transfer to another browser.

guide_section_obsidian_downloads_html.message:
#Obsidian & Downloads

#Obsidian Integration

Enable this to show the Send to Obsidian button and Obsidian context menu actions. Requires the Advanced Obsidian URI community plugin.

  • Vault Name — leave blank for the default vault.
  • Folder Name — supports text substitutions (e.g. {date:YYYY-MM-DD}/).
Note: Images sent via Obsidian stay as remote links. The extension does not place attachment files inside the vault.

#Download Mode

ModeDescription
Downloads API (recommended)Uses the browser's download API. Supports subfolders, image downloads, and Save As dialogs.
Content LinkFalls back to a content link for downloading. More limited — disables image downloads and subfolders.

Show Save As Dialog

Forces the browser's Save As dialog for every download. Not recommended when Download Images is on.

#Image Downloads

When enabled (Downloads API mode only), images are downloaded alongside the Markdown file with local references.

Image Filename Prefix

A prefix or subfolder for downloaded images. Uses text substitutions. Including a / creates a subfolder. Default: {pageTitle}/.

guide_section_permissions_privacy_html.message:
#Permissions & Privacy

MarkSnip processes pages entirely within your browser. It does not send page content to any external server. This section explains what browser permissions MarkSnip uses, why they are needed, and when they are actually exercised.

#Site Access

MarkSnip requests access to web pages so it can clip pages across the web when you trigger a clip. This covers popup clipping, keyboard shortcuts, context-menu actions, multi-tab link actions, and batch processing of URLs opened in new tabs.

  • Only active when you clip — MarkSnip does not monitor browsing activity or read pages in the background.
  • Processed locally — page content is converted to Markdown inside your browser and is never sent to a remote service.
  • Managed by the browser — you can review this permission in your browser’s extension settings at any time.

#Other Core Permissions

Beyond site access, MarkSnip uses a small set of standard browser permissions to make its main features work. These permissions support local browser actions; they do not change the fact that clipping stays local.

PermissionWhy MarkSnip uses itWhen it matters
activeTabLets MarkSnip work with the page you explicitly invoked it on.Opening the popup or using a shortcut on the current tab.
downloadsSaves Markdown files, ZIP exports, and downloaded images through the browser.Downloading clips or batch exports.
clipboardWriteWrites generated Markdown to your clipboard.Using Copy actions.
storageRemembers your settings, templates, themes, and feature toggles.Whenever you save or restore settings.
contextMenusAdds right-click actions for pages, selections, links, images, and tabs.When context menus are enabled in settings.
scriptingInjects MarkSnip’s capture helpers into the page at clip time.Whenever MarkSnip reads page content.
offscreenUses a hidden document for Manifest V3 clipboard and download workflows in Chromium browsers.Behind the scenes during some copy and download actions.

#Agent Bridge Permission

The Agent Bridge feature requires an additional browser permission called Native Messaging. This is an optional permission — MarkSnip works perfectly without it.

  • What it does — lets MarkSnip talk to a small local companion program installed on your machine.
  • Local only — communication stays on your computer. No data leaves your machine.
  • When it’s used — only while Agent Bridge is enabled. Turning the toggle off stops all native messaging activity.
  • How to grant — enable the Agent Bridge toggle in Settings. MarkSnip will show an explanation before the browser’s permission prompt appears.
  • How to revoke — once granted, this permission cannot be selectively removed. To fully revoke it, you must reinstall the extension. Turning off the Agent Bridge toggle stops all native messaging activity without needing to revoke the permission.
Note: If you deny the permission, regular clipping is completely unaffected. You can always try again later.

guide_section_troubleshooting_html.message:
#Troubleshooting / FAQ

The popup shows "Processing page..." and nothing happens

Some pages block content scripts. Try refreshing the page, or check that MarkSnip has permission to access the site. Internal browser pages (chrome://, about:) cannot be clipped.

Images are not downloading

Make sure Download Mode is set to "Downloads API" and the Images toggle is enabled. Content Link mode does not support image downloads.

Send to Obsidian is not visible

Enable Obsidian Integration in Settings. You also need the Advanced Obsidian URI community plugin installed in Obsidian.

Context menu items are missing

Check that Enable Context Menus is turned on in Downloads settings.

Keyboard shortcuts don't work

Another extension may have claimed the same shortcut. Open your browser's extension shortcut settings to reassign keys.

Markdown output looks wrong

Check the Markdown Options to adjust heading style, link style, code block style, and escape settings to match your workflow.

guide_section_text_substitutions_html.message:
#Custom Text Substitutions

The Title Template, Downloads Subfolder, Front/Back Templates, Image Filename Prefix, and Obsidian Folder Name all support these variables:

Article Metadata

VariableDescription
{title}Article title (as determined by Readability)
{pageTitle}Title of the actual page (<title> tag)
{length}Length of the article in characters
{excerpt}Article description or short excerpt
{byline}Author metadata
{dir}Content direction (e.g. ltr)
{baseURI}Parsed document/base URI
{pageURL} / {tabURL}Address-bar URL of the tab
{keywords}Meta keywords, comma-separated

URL Components

VariableDescription
{origin}Scheme + domain + port
{host}Hostname + port
{hostname}Domain only
{port}Port number
{protocol}Protocol with trailing :
{pathname}URL path
{search}Query string including ?
{hash}Fragment identifier including #

All URL components are also available prefixed with page (e.g. {pageOrigin}, {pageHostname}).

Date/Time

Use {date:FORMAT} with Moment.js formatting tokens. Example: {date:YYYY-MM-DD}.

Meta Tags

Any <meta> tag value can be referenced, e.g. {og:image}, {og:description}.

Parameterizations

Change the casing of any text variable (except {date} and {keywords}) by appending a colon and style:

SyntaxStyleExample
{variable:pascal}PascalCaseDifferentTypesOfCasing
{variable:camel}camelCasedifferentTypesOfCasing
{variable:kebab}kebab-casedifferent-types-of-casing
{variable:snake}snake_casedifferent_types_of_casing
{variable:lowercase}lowercaseall lowercase
{variable:uppercase}UPPERCASEALL UPPERCASE
Note: Not all websites provide all metadata values. Missing values are replaced with empty strings.
